name: CPU Cache & Memory Hierarchy
description: The memory hierarchy serves CPU accesses from the fastest level that holds the data: L1 is checked first, then L2/L3; on a last-level miss the line is fetched from main memory, evicting and writing back a dirty victim if needed, then filling the cache and updating replacement state before returning data.
mermaid:
graph TD
    N1["CPU memory access"]
    N2["Check L1 cache"]
    N3{"L1 hit?"}
    N4["Return data (fast)"]
    N5["Check L2 / L3"]
    N6{"LLC hit?"}
    N7["Fetch from memory"]
    N8{"Eviction needed?"}
    N9["Write back dirty line"]
    N10["Fill cache line"]
    N11["Update replacement"]
    N12["Data to CPU"]

    N1 --> N2
    N2 --> N3
    N3 -->|yes| N4
    N4 --> N12
    N3 -->|no| N5
    N5 --> N6
    N6 -->|yes| N10
    N6 -->|no| N7
    N7 --> N8
    N8 -->|yes| N9
    N9 --> N10
    N8 -->|no| N10
    N10 --> N11
    N11 --> N12

    style N1 fill:#ff6b6b,color:#fff
    style N2 fill:#51cf66,color:#fff
    style N3 fill:#74c0fc,color:#fff
    style N4 fill:#51cf66,color:#fff
    style N5 fill:#51cf66,color:#fff
    style N6 fill:#74c0fc,color:#fff
    style N7 fill:#51cf66,color:#fff
    style N8 fill:#74c0fc,color:#fff
    style N9 fill:#51cf66,color:#fff
    style N10 fill:#74c0fc,color:#fff
    style N11 fill:#51cf66,color:#fff
    style N12 fill:#b197fc,color:#fff

sources.title: Computer Architecture: A Quantitative Approach (6th ed.)
sources.authors: Hennessy, J. L.; Patterson, D. A.

notes: Source-grounded extraction (v2): node content derived from cited protocol literature with per-node provenance; topology reflects the actual procedure.

flowchartStandard.name: source_grounded_extraction_v2
flowchartStandard.sourceGrounding: Nodes, decisions, branches, and loops were extracted from the cited sources; each node carries a sourceRef to its supporting citation. Awaiting human spot-check before verified=true.
